$comment: TEMPLATE — the fleet config lives in your ops home (KAANHA_HOME or ~/.claude/kaanha), which the kaanha-dev hub bootstraps on first run; fill in YOUR targets there. Every kaanha-agents squad reads this file. Absolute paths are fine: this file describes ONE machine's fleet. repos[] is auto-synced from the dev-hub registry. Replace every <...> placeholder.
$ownership: By listing a site or repo here you attest you OWN it or have written permission to test it. The security-auditor and site-sentinel squads probe these targets; they will skip any target whose ownership is unattested. Never add a third party's site.

sites.name: <your-site-name>
sites.brandSpec: BRAND.md or STYLE.md — the file holding your voice/design rules, or omit
sites.notes: Framework/host + anything the squads should know. Name any public API/agent endpoint so squads probe it gently.

e2e.command: <the repo's e2e command, e.g. npm run test:e2e — omit this whole block to skip flow-testing>
e2e.localPreview: <build + preview command, e.g. npm run build && npm run preview>

flows: describe each critical user flow in one line — the flow-tester reports any with no covering spec

repos: <absolute path to each repo the fleet should watch/audit>
backlog: <absolute path to builder-backlog.md — copy the template beside this file>
reports: <absolute path to the directory where squads write their HTML reports>
